ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When the Occurrence occurs,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the Inevitable Event takes place,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There is, at its occurrence, no denial.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then no one can deny it has come.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It will bring down [some] and raise up [others].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It will debase ˹some˺ and elevate ˹others˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When the earth is shaken with convulsion
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the earth will be violently shaken,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the mountains are broken down, crumbling
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the mountains will be crushed to pieces,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And become dust dispersing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: becoming scattered ˹particles of˺ dust,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you become [of] three kinds:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: you will ˹all˺ be ˹divided into˺ three groups:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then the companions of the right - what are the companions of the right?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the people of the right, how ˹blessed˺ will they be;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the companions of the left - what are the companions of the left?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the people of the left, how ˹miserable˺ will they be;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the forerunners, the forerunners -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the foremost ˹in faith˺ will be the foremost ˹in Paradise˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those are the ones brought near [to Allah]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They are the ones nearest ˹to Allah˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In the Gardens of Pleasure,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in the Gardens of Bliss.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A [large] company of the former peoples
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be˺ a multitude from earlier generations

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And a few of the later peoples,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and a few from later generations.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On thrones woven [with ornament],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹All will be˺ on jewelled thrones,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Reclining on them, facing each other.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: reclining face to face.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There will circulate among them young boys made eternal
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will be waited on by eternal youths

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: With vessels, pitchers and a cup [of wine] from a flowing spring -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with cups, pitchers, and a drink ˹of pure wine˺ from a flowing stream,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No headache will they have therefrom, nor will they be intoxicated -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that will cause them neither headache nor intoxication.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And fruit of what they select
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will also be served˺ any fruit they choose

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the meat of fowl, from whatever they desire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and meat from any bird they desire.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [for them are] fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹they will have˺ maidens with gorgeous eyes,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The likenesses of pearls well-protected,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: like pristine pearls,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As reward for what they used to do.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹all˺ as a reward for what they used to do.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يَسۡمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغۡوٗا وَلَا تَأۡثِيمًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yasmaAAoona feeha laghwan wala tatheeman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will not hear therein ill speech or commission of sin -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There they will never hear any idle or sinful talk—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Only a saying: "Peace, peace."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: only good and virtuous speech.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The companions of the right - what are the companions of the right?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And the people of the right—how ˹blessed˺ will they be!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be] among lote trees with thorns removed
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be˺ amid thornless lote trees,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [banana] trees layered [with fruit]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: clusters of bananas,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And shade extended
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: extended shade,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And water poured out
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: flowing water,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And fruit, abundant [and varied],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: abundant fruit—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Neither limited [to season] nor forbidden,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: never out of season nor forbidden—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [upon] beds raised high.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and elevated furnishings.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We have produced the women of Paradise in a [new] creation
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We will have perfectly created their mates,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And made them virgins,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: making them virgins,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Devoted [to their husbands] and of equal age,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: loving and of equal age,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For the companions of the right [who are]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: for the people of the right,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A company of the former peoples
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹who will be˺ a multitude from earlier generations

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And a company of the later peoples.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and a multitude from later generations.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the companions of the left - what are the companions of the left?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And the people of the left—how ˹miserable˺ will they be!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be] in scorching fire and scalding water
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be˺ in scorching heat and boiling water,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And a shade of black smoke,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in the shade of black smoke,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Neither cool nor beneficial.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: neither cool nor refreshing.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed they were, before that, indulging in affluence,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, before this ˹torment˺ they were spoiled by luxury,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they used to persist in the great violation,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and persisted in the worst of sin.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَانُواْ يَقُولُونَ أَئِذَا مِتۡنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابٗا وَعِظَٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبۡعُوثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakanoo yaqooloona aitha mitna wakunna turaban waAAithaman ainna lamabAAoothoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they used to say, "When we die and become dust and bones, are we indeed to be resurrected?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They used to ask ˹mockingly˺, “When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And our forefathers [as well]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And our forefathers as well?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, the former and the later peoples
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Most certainly, earlier and later generations

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Are to be gathered together for the appointment of a known Day."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: will surely be gathered ˹together˺ for the appointed Day.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed you, O those astray [who are] deniers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then you, O misguided deniers,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Will be eating from trees of zaqqum
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: will certainly eat from ˹the fruit of˺ the trees of Zaqqûm,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And filling with it your bellies
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: filling up ˹your˺ bellies with it.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And drinking on top of it from scalding water
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then on top of that you will drink boiling water—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And will drink as the drinking of thirsty camels.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and you will drink ˹it˺ like thirsty camels do.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That is their accommodation on the Day of Recompense.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This will be their accommodation on the Day of Judgment.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have created you, so why do you not believe?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is We Who created you. Will you not then believe ˹in resurrection˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have you seen that which you emit?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you considered what you ejaculate?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is it you who creates it, or are We the Creator?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it you who create ˹a child out of˺ it, or is it We Who do so?

ayahs.textArabic: نَحۡنُ قَدَّرۡنَا بَيۡنَكُمُ ٱلۡمَوۡتَ وَمَا نَحۡنُ بِمَسۡبُوقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Nahnu qaddarna baynakumu almawta wama nahnu bimasbooqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have decreed death among you, and We are not to be outdone
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have ordained death for ˹all of˺ you, and We cannot be prevented

ayahs.textArabic: عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ أَمۡثَٰلَكُمۡ وَنُنشِئَكُمۡ فِي مَا لَا تَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AAala an nubaddila amthalakum wanunshiakum fee ma la taAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In that We will change your likenesses and produce you in that [form] which you do not know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: from transforming and recreating you in forms unknown to you.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتُمُ ٱلنَّشۡأَةَ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ فَلَوۡلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad AAalimtumu alnnashata aloola falawla tathakkaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you have already known the first creation, so will you not remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You already know how you were first created. Will you not then be mindful?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And have you seen that [seed] which you sow?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you considered what you sow?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is it you who makes it grow, or are We the grower?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it you who cause it to grow, or is it We Who do so?

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ نَشَآءُ لَجَعَلۡنَٰهُ حُطَٰمٗا فَظَلۡتُمۡ تَفَكَّهُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law nashao lajaAAalnahu hutaman fathaltum tafakkahoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: If We willed, We could make it [dry] debris, and you would remain in wonder,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If We willed, We could simply reduce this ˹harvest˺ to chaff, leaving you to lament,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Saying], "Indeed, we are [now] in debt;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “We have truly suffered a ˹great˺ loss.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, we have been deprived."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, we have been deprived ˹of our livelihood˺.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And have you seen the water that you drink?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you considered the water you drink?

ayahs.textArabic: ءَأَنتُمۡ أَنزَلۡتُمُوهُ مِنَ ٱلۡمُزۡنِ أَمۡ نَحۡنُ ٱلۡمُنزِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aantum anzaltumoohu mina almuzni am nahnu almunziloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is it you who brought it down from the clouds, or is it We who bring it down?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it you who bring it down from the clouds, or is it We Who do so?

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ نَشَآءُ جَعَلۡنَٰهُ أُجَاجٗا فَلَوۡلَا تَشۡكُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law nashao jaAAalnahu ojajan falawla tashkuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: If We willed, We could make it bitter, so why are you not grateful?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If We willed, We could make it salty. Will you not then give thanks?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And have you seen the fire that you ignite?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you considered the fire you kindle?

ayahs.textArabic: ءَأَنتُمۡ أَنشَأۡتُمۡ شَجَرَتَهَآ أَمۡ نَحۡنُ ٱلۡمُنشِـُٔونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aantum anshatum shajarataha am nahnu almunshioona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is it you who produced its tree, or are We the producer?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it you who produce its trees, or is it We Who do so?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have made it a reminder and provision for the travelers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have made it ˹as˺ a reminder ˹of the Hellfire˺ and a provision for the travellers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then I swear by the setting of the stars,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So I do swear by the positions of the stars—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, it is an oath - if you could know - [most] great.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and this, if only you knew, is indeed a great oath—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it is a noble Qur'an
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that this is truly a noble Quran,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In a Register well-protected;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in a well-preserved Record,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: None touch it except the purified.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: touched by none except the purified ˹angels˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It is] a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It is˺ a revelation from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then is it to this statement that you are indifferent
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: How can you then take this message lightly,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And make [the thanks for] your provision that you deny [the Provider]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and repay ˹Allah for˺ your provisions with denial?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then why, when the soul at death reaches the throat
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Why then ˹are you helpless˺ when the soul ˹of a dying person˺ reaches ˹their˺ throat,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you are at that time looking on -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: while you are looking on?

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَحۡنُ أَقۡرَبُ إِلَيۡهِ مِنكُمۡ وَلَٰكِن لَّا تُبۡصِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanahnu aqrabu ilayhi minkum walakin la tubsiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Our angels are nearer to him than you, but you do not see -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We are nearer to such a person than you, but you cannot see.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then why do you not, if you are not to be recompensed,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, if you are not subject to Our Will ˹as you claim˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Bring it back, if you should be truthful?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: bring that soul back, if what you say is true.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if the deceased was of those brought near to Allah,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So, if the deceased is one of those brought near ˹to Us˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then [for him is] rest and bounty and a garden of pleasure.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then ˹such a person will have˺ serenity, fragrance, and a Garden of Bliss.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنۡ أَصۡحَٰبِ ٱلۡيَمِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamma in kana min ashabi alyameeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if he was of the companions of the right,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And if the deceased is one of the people of the right,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then [the angels will say], "Peace for you; [you are] from the companions of the right."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then ˹they will be told,˺ “Greetings to you from the people of the right.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُكَذِّبِينَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamma in kana mina almukaththibeena alddalleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But if he was of the deniers [who were] astray,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But if such person is one of the misguided deniers,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then [for him is] accommodation of scalding water
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then their accommodation will be boiling water ˹to drink˺

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And burning in Hellfire
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and burning in Hellfire.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, this is the true certainty,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, this is the absolute truth.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.
